name: Space Systems Engineering Lab (SSEL)
relevance_explanation: A premier 7,000-square-foot facility for satellite design, fabrication, and operation, hosting the Satellite Operations Center and ground station.

name: Midshipmen Space Operations Team (MSOPS)
relevance_explanation: A team of midshipmen who participate in space operations, including operating satellites and managing ISS-related hardware.

name: i5 Club
relevance_explanation: The Institute for Applied Space Policy and Strategy (i5) educates midshipmen on space policy, doctrine, and warfighting capabilities.

name: USNA Rocket Team (Navy Rockets)
relevance_explanation: Midshipmen design, build, and fly high-power rockets and payloads for the NASA University Student Launch competition.

name: RaVision
relevance_explanation: Robotics and Autonomous Vehicles in Support of the Navy (RA-VISION) conducts research in autonomous systems and human-robot interaction.

name: ARTeMis Lab
relevance_explanation: The Aerial Robotics Testing and Mission Lab is a facility for developing and testing autonomous aerial vehicles.

name: Naval Academy Small Satellite Program (NASSP)
relevance_explanation: An undergraduate research program where midshipmen design, build, test, and operate small satellites (CubeSats).

name: Astronomy Club
relevance_explanation: A student organization focused on observational astronomy, utilizing the Class of 1941 Observatory for research and casual observing.

name: AIAA Student Chapter
relevance_explanation: The USNA branch of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics supports student interest and professional development in aerospace engineering.

name: USNA STEM Center
relevance_explanation: Promotes STEM education and outreach through workshops and programs like SeaPerch underwater robotics and Summer STEM.

name: Powered Flight Program (PFP)
relevance_explanation: A summer training program that introduces midshipmen to aviation through formal flight instruction and simulations.

name: MIDSTAR Satellite Project
relevance_explanation: A historical project within the Small Satellite Program that developed the MIDSTAR-1 satellite launched in 2007.

name: Association of Naval Aviation (ANA) - Two Block Fox Squadron
relevance_explanation: A professional and alumni-focused organization supporting naval aviation, with an active squadron at the Naval Academy.

name: Rotor Lab
relevance_explanation: Houses a full-size helicopter rotor test cell for undergraduate research in rotary-wing aircraft.

name: Weapons, Robotics, and Control Engineering (WRC) Department
relevance_explanation: The primary academic department at USNA for robotics and autonomous systems education and research.

name: Navy Formula SAE Team
relevance_explanation: A midshipman team that designs and builds formula-style racecars, focusing on advanced vehicle control and engineering.
